name: Seafood Frittata
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, melt 1/4 cup clarified butter over medium heat.
2. Add 1/2 cup salmon pieces, 1/2 cup scallops, 1/2 cup rockfish pieces (firm white fish), 1/3 cup shrimp, 1/3 cup sliced mushrooms, and 1/4 cup thinly sliced onions. Cook for a few minutes until the vegetables are softened.
3. Stir in 1 teaspoon dried oregano and 2 tablespoons white wine. Allow the mixture to simmer for about 5 minutes or until most of the liquid has evaporated.
4. In a bowl, beat 2 eggs. Add 1/4 cup half-and-half and 2 shakes Tabasco sauce. Mix well.
5. Slice the tomatoes into thin pieces and set aside.
6. Pour the egg mixture over the seafood in the skillet. Gently stir to combine.
7. Cook the frittata on low heat for about 10-12 minutes, or until the edges are set but the center is still slightly runny.
8. Remove from heat and sprinkle with shredded cheese.
9. Place the skillet in a preheated oven at 425°F (220°C) for an additional 4-5 minutes, or until the frittata is fully cooked.
10. Transfer to a serving plate and garnish with a dollop of sour cream.
